rationale: The one substantive ground-truth concern (whether the test verifies the new reduce_scatter parameter) is touched by X, which explicitly inspects the FakeGroup's reduce_scatter conditional and the corresponding hasattr assertions, while Y never mentions reduce_scatter, the FakeGroup mock, or the test file changes at all. X also produces five concrete, line-anchored, evidence-backed suggestions (dtype mismatch vs upstream int32, missing cfg=1 test case, docstring/comment updates) that are all grounded in the diff, whereas Y's single non-blocking comment is vaguer and its 'APPROVE' justification includes a shakier claim (device_communicator defaulting to True upstream) that sits oddly with the PR's whole premise. X is clearly more actionable and grounded even though both avoid fabrication overall.
